_comment: Auto-generated by scripts/sync_schemas.py from the ingext_schema repo. Do not edit by hand. Keys are KQL table names as returned by list_data_tables.

AzureAuditLogs.description: Schema for the Azure Active Directory (Entra ID) AuditLogs table. Contains system activity records for user and group management, application and directory changes, and other administrative operations. Query the table as AuditLogs. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/reference/tables/auditlogs

queries.description: Returns all directory group, role, and permission changes initiated by the specified user within the selected time range. This report helps investigators understand what actions the user performed ...

Returns all directory group, role, and permission changes where the specified user was the target within the selected time range. This report helps investigators determine whether the user's access...

AzureSigninLogs.description: Schema for the Azure Active Directory (Entra ID) SigninLogs table. Contains interactive and non-interactive user sign-in events, including authentication, conditional access, risk, device, and location details. Query the table as SigninLogs. Dynamic fields hinted as JSON (e.g. DeviceDetail, LocationDetails, Status) support dot/bracket access directly; plain String fields holding JSON text require parse_json() first. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/reference/tables/signinlogs

queries.description: Returns the sign-in activity history for the specified user within the selected time range. This report provides a detailed timeline of the user's authentication events in Azure Active Directory, h...

A faceted breakdown of Azure AD sign-ins over the past 7 days. Renames the key sign-in fields to short aliases, then produces value distributions across app, authentication requirement, client app,...

CortexAlert.description: PaloAlto Cortex XDR alert, imported by Ingext from the Cortex XDR API (Get Alerts / alerts endpoint). Each record is one detection raised by a Cortex detector (XDR Agent local analysis, BIOC, correlation, firewall, etc.) on one endpoint: the alert identity and classification, the affected agent/host, the triggering rule, resolution/matching status, and a nested 'events' array holding the raw causality-graph event(s) that produced the alert (actor/causality/OS process lineage, file/registry/network actions, and firewall fields). Alerts are grouped into a CortexIncident via case_id (= the incident's incident_id). The object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested fields use dot notation (e.g. events[0].actor_process_image_name) and the 'events' array requires mv-expand to enumerate. timestamp is parsed from detection_timestamp.

queries.description: Cortex XDR malware/threat alerts grouped by endpoint over the last 7 days. For each affected host it summarizes how many alerts fired, the worst severity seen, the distinct alert names and categori...

CortexIncident.description: PaloAlto Cortex XDR incident, imported by Ingext from the Cortex XDR API (Get Incidents / incidents endpoint). Each record is one incident: a group of related CortexAlert records sharing a common story, scored and summarized. It carries the incident identity and status, severity (effective and the per-severity alert counts), the aggregated/predicted/manual risk scores, the affected hosts and users, the contributing alert categories and sources, MITRE mapping, assignment/resolution metadata, and a deep link to the Cortex console. Alerts join to their incident via CortexAlert.case_id == incident_id. The object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so array fields (hosts, users, alert_categories, incident_sources) require mv-expand. timestamp is parsed from creation_time.

queries.description: Open Cortex XDR incidents triage: lists incidents that are still 'new' (not yet investigated or resolved), with their effective severity and risk score, the contributing alert categories and per-se...

Meraki.description: Unified schema for Cisco Meraki log events. Use 'applicableFacility' to determine if a field is valid for the queried facility. The 'facility' field identifies the event category; most network-flow facilities share a common 5-tuple (src/dst/sport/dport/protocol) plus the client mac.

NetworkFortigateEvent.description: Schema for FortiGate event and UTM logs (type=event and type=utm). Fields above _fields are shared across FortiGate log families (see FortiGate Traffic for matching definitions); subtype-specific columns are held in the _fields dynamic JSON bag keyed by logid. Reference: https://docs.fortinet.com/document/fortigate/7.6.6/fortios-log-message-reference/357866/log-message-fields

NetworkFortigateTraffic.description: Schema for FortiGate firewall traffic (forward session) logs (type=traffic). One row per session. Subtype-specific keys not modeled as columns are held in the _fields dynamic JSON bag. Reference: https://docs.fortinet.com/document/fortigate/7.6.6/fortios-log-message-reference/357866/log-message-fields

queries.description: The top 10 source IPs by total bandwidth (bytes sent plus received) through the FortiGate over the past hour. Uses the per-interval sent/received deltas rather than the cumulative session counters,...

Office365.description: Unified schema for Microsoft 365 audit events. Use 'applicableWorkloads' to determine if a field is valid for the queried workload.

ZscalerDNS.description: Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA) DNS control log, streamed by Ingext from a Zscaler NSS DNS-log feed. Each record is one DNS request/response the Zscaler cloud firewall's DNS control processed: who/where (user, department, location, client device), the query (name and record type) and answer, the client source and Zscaler server destination addresses, the DNS category, and the policy actions and matched rules applied separately to the request and the response. Field semantics follow the Zscaler "NSS Feed Output Format: DNS Logs" specification. Numeric fields arrive as JSON strings; cast with toint() before arithmetic. timestamp is parsed from the 'datetime' field.

queries.description: Zscaler DNS control requests that were blocked over the last 24 hours, grouped by queried domain and category. A request is treated as blocked when either the request or the response action was 'Bl...

ZscalerFirewall.description: Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA) cloud firewall session log, streamed by Ingext from a Zscaler NSS firewall-log feed. Each record is one network session (or an aggregate of sessions) the Zscaler cloud firewall processed: who/where (user, department, location, client device, tunnel), the full client/server address pairs across NAT (client source/dest and server source/dest IP:port), the firewall verdict and matched rule, the network service/application, byte counts and durations, and security context (IP category, threat, destination country). Field semantics follow the Zscaler "NSS Feed Output Format: Firewall Logs" specification. Most numeric fields arrive as JSON strings, so cast with toint()/tolong()/todouble() before arithmetic. timestamp is parsed from the 'datetime' field.

queries.description: Zscaler cloud firewall sessions that were blocked over the last 24 hours, summarized by the matched rule and destination. Shows the blocked destination server, port and country, the network applica...

ZscalerWeb.description: Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA) web proxy transaction log, streamed by Ingext from a Zscaler NSS (Nanolog Streaming Service) web-log feed. Each record is one HTTP/HTTPS transaction a user's device made through the Zscaler cloud proxy: who/where (login, department, location, client device), the request (URL, host, method, user-agent), the proxy verdict (action, applied rule), the response (status code, sizes, timing), and security/classification context (URL category class, cloud-app name/class, malware/threat, DLP, risk score). Field semantics follow the Zscaler "NSS Feed Output Format: Web Logs" specification. Most numeric fields arrive as JSON strings, so cast with toint()/tolong()/todouble() before arithmetic. timestamp is parsed from the 'time' field.

queries.description: Blocked Zscaler web transactions over the last 24 hours, grouped by URL category and the policy rule that blocked them. Surfaces which categories and rules are driving denials and how many distinct...

behavior.description: Fluency behavior timeline event (notable event). Each record represents one behavior detected for an entity (the "key") by a behavior rule, along with a risk contribution and first-seen/recurrence history. Rule-specific details are carried in the attributes array and its map form, AttributeMap.

queries.description: Lists email threat-intelligence behaviors detected by the Security Compliance Center mail-data rule over the past 7 days, surfacing messages with a "Phish" verdict. The rule-specific details (verdi...

blackkiteFinding.description: Black Kite third-party cyber-risk finding collected by Ingext from the Black Kite API. Each record is one vulnerability/control finding discovered on a monitored vendor's external attack surface: the affected asset (domain/subdomain, IP, port, product), the associated CVE with its CVSS/CWE/EPSS scoring and CISA KEV status, severity and lifecycle status, and any linked remediation ticket. The object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested objects use dot notation (e.g. Ticket.Status) and arrays (References, Tickets) require mv-expand.

queries.description: Active, high-priority Black Kite findings on monitored vendor attack surfaces: keeps open findings that are Critical/High, in CISA KEV, or have a high EPSS exploitation probability, and flags any w...

gsuiteGroup.description: Google Workspace (GSuite) directory group resource snapshot collected by Ingext from the Google Admin SDK Directory API (resource kind admin#directory#group). Each record is the current state of one group: name/email, description, alias addresses, direct member count, and the expanded member list (each member with email, role, status, and type). The group object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested objects are accessed with dot notation and arrays require mv-expand (e.g. members, nonEditableAliases).

queries.description: Google Workspace group membership inventory: lists groups with their direct member count and a breakdown of owners/managers and any external (non-USER / EXTERNAL) members expanded from the member l...

gsuiteUser.description: Google Workspace (GSuite) directory user resource snapshot collected by Ingext from the Google Admin SDK Directory API (resource kind admin#directory#user). Each record is the current state of one directory user: profile/name, primary and alias email addresses, org unit placement, account/security status, 2-Step Verification enforcement, and recovery info. The user object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested objects are accessed with dot notation (e.g. name.fullName) and arrays require mv-expand (e.g. emails, languages).

queries.description: Google Workspace users for whom 2-Step Verification is enforced but who have not yet enrolled, plus super/delegated admins, ordered to surface privileged and unprotected accounts first. Active (non...

office365Application.description: Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (Azure AD) application registration snapshot collected by Ingext. Each record is the current state of one app registration: identity, credentials (client secrets and certificates), requested API permissions, redirect/auth configuration, and publisher/governance settings. The application object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested objects are accessed with dot notation (e.g. web.implicitGrantSettings.enableIdTokenIssuance) and arrays require mv-expand. Useful for app-governance and credential-hygiene reporting (e.g. over-privileged apps, expiring secrets).

queries.description: Finds application registrations that request high-privilege Microsoft Graph (or other API) application permissions (type == "Role", i.e. app-only consent that does not require a signed-in user). Fl...

office365Device.description: Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (Azure AD) device resource snapshot collected by Ingext. Each record is the current state of one registered/joined device: identity, OS and hardware, compliance and management status, trust/join type, on-prem sync, and registration timestamps. The device object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested objects are accessed with dot notation (e.g. extensionAttributes.extensionAttribute1) and arrays require mv-expand. Useful for device-inventory, stale-device, and compliance reporting.

queries.description: Finds enabled devices that have not signed in for more than 90 days (approximateLastSignInDateTime older than the threshold), i.e. stale device registrations that should be reviewed and cleaned up....

office365Group.description: Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (Azure AD) group resource snapshot collected by Ingext. Each record is the current state of one group: identity, mail settings, group type and security flags, membership (and dynamic membership rule), lifecycle timestamps, and on-premises sync details. The group object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so arrays (members, proxyAddresses, groupTypes) require mv-expand. Useful for membership audits, group-inventory, and security/role-assignable group reporting.

queries.description: Lists Microsoft 365 ("Unified") groups with their member counts and key settings (visibility, mail/security flags), ordered by size. Useful for group inventory, spotting empty or oversized collabor...

office365InstalledApp.description: Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (Azure AD) service principal (enterprise application) snapshot collected by Ingext. Each record is the tenant-local instance of an application — including first-party Microsoft apps, gallery/SaaS apps, and the service principals backing your own app registrations. This is distinct from office365Application (the app registration / global definition); office365InstalledApp is what is actually installed/consented in the tenant. The object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so arrays (servicePrincipalNames, oauth2PermissionScopes) require mv-expand. Useful for enterprise-app inventory, consent/credential review, and spotting disabled or credentialed service principals.

queries.description: Inventory of enabled enterprise applications (service principals) in the tenant, classifying each as a Microsoft first-party app or a third-party / own-tenant app (via appOwnerOrganizationId) and c...

office365User.description: Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (Azure AD) user resource snapshot collected by Ingext. Each record is the current state of one directory user: profile, account status, assigned licenses and service plans, group and role memberships, registered authentication methods, and MFA/SSPR registration details. The user object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested objects are accessed with dot notation (e.g. userRegistration.isMfaRegistered) and arrays require mv-expand.

queries.description: Lists all administrative users (userRegistration.isAdmin == true) and their MFA and SSPR registration status, highlighting privileged accounts that are not protected by MFA. This is a key hygiene r...

qualysHost.description: Qualys host asset snapshot collected by Ingext from the Qualys API (host asset inventory, typically VMDR / Cloud Agent). Each record is the current state of one tracked host: identity (name/FQDN/DNS/NetBIOS, Qualys host IDs), network address, operating system and hardware, the last vulnerability / compliance scan and boot times, and — for agent-tracked hosts — a nested agentInfo object describing the Cloud Agent (version, modules, check-in, status, geolocation). The object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested fields are accessed with dot notation (e.g. agentInfo.status). timestamp is parsed from the 'created' field and _key is mapped to the 'name' field.

queries.description: Qualys host inventory breakdown: counts tracked hosts by operating system, tracking method, and Cloud Agent status to give a quick picture of fleet composition and how assets are being monitored. U...

Qualys host hygiene review: surfaces tracked hosts that are falling out of coverage — not scanned for vulnerabilities recently, or running a Cloud Agent that has not checked in lately. Flags each h...

sentinelOneAgent.description: SentinelOne endpoint agent resource snapshot collected by Ingext from the SentinelOne management API (agents inventory). Each record is the current state of one installed agent/endpoint: hardware and OS, agent version and health, protection/mitigation posture, scan and threat status, network/management connectivity, site/group placement, and last logged-in user. The object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested objects are accessed with dot notation (e.g. activeDirectory.computerDistinguishedName) and arrays require mv-expand.

queries.description: SentinelOne endpoint posture review: surfaces agents that need attention — infected or with active threats, not in 'protect' mitigation mode, out-of-date agent version, or stale (not active and off...

sentinelOneApplication.description: SentinelOne application-inventory resource snapshot collected by Ingext from the SentinelOne management API (installed applications). Each record is one application installed on one endpoint: application identity (name, version, publisher, signed/risk), install/update timestamps, size, and a denormalized set of agent.* fields identifying the endpoint it is installed on. Because each row is per (agent, application), the same application appears once per endpoint it is installed on. The object is stored as a dynamic JSON document; use mv-expand for any nested arrays.

queries.description: SentinelOne software inventory rollup: aggregates installed applications across endpoints to show, per application+version, how many endpoints have it, whether it is unsigned, and its highest repor...

sentinelOneThreat.description: SentinelOne threat/detection resource snapshot collected by Ingext from the SentinelOne management API (threats). Each record is one detected threat on one endpoint: the threat file/process and its classification, analyst verdict and mitigation/incident status, the behavioral indicators that fired, and denormalized agent context at both detection time (agentDetectionInfo) and the current/real-time state (agentRealtimeInfo). The object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested objects use dot notation (e.g. threatInfo.classification) and arrays (indicators, mitigationStatus) require mv-expand.

queries.description: Open SentinelOne threats triage: lists detections that are still unresolved and not mitigated, with the affected endpoint/user, classification and confidence, the analyst verdict, and the set of be...
